name: T-Mobile 600
note: 600 MHz (Band 71) — T-Mobile's nationwide low-band 5G 'Extended Range', won at the 2017 broadcast incentive auction. Low frequencies reach far and punch into buildings.

name: AT&T 700
note: Lower 700 MHz — AT&T's long-reach LTE/5G low band.

name: Verizon 700
note: Upper 700 MHz C-block (Band 13) — Verizon's original nationwide LTE backbone.

name: AT&T FirstNet
note: Band 14 (700 MHz) — FirstNet, the dedicated public-safety broadband network AT&T builds and operates for first responders.

name: GPS L1
note: 1575.42 MHz GPS L1 — operated by the US Space Force; the civilian signal every phone, car and aircraft navigates by.

name: Iridium
note: 1.6 GHz L-band — Iridium's 66-satellite constellation for satellite phones and the off-grid messaging some new phones and trackers use.

name: Dish AWS-4
note: AWS-4 (2 GHz) — Dish Network spectrum behind the Boost Mobile / Dish 5G build-out.

name: SiriusXM
note: 2.3 GHz SDARS — SiriusXM satellite radio, the sole licensee of this band.

name: Globalstar n53
note: Band n53 (2.4 GHz) — Globalstar; the spectrum licensed to Apple's iPhone Emergency SOS via satellite.

name: T-Mobile 2.5 GHz
note: 2.5 GHz (Band 41) — T-Mobile's mid-band 5G workhorse, inherited from Sprint (originally Clearwire WiMAX).

name: AT&T 3.45 GHz
note: 3.45 GHz — auctioned from federal radar in 2022; AT&T and Dish are major holders for mid-band 5G.

name: Verizon C-band
note: C-band (3.7–3.98 GHz) — cleared from satellite in the 2021 'C-band auction'; Verizon won the largest share for its mid-band 5G Ultra Wideband.

name: Dish / DirecTV
note: 12.2–12.7 GHz DBS — direct-broadcast satellite TV (Dish Network and DirecTV), the little dish on the roof.

name: Starlink uplink
note: 14 GHz Ku-band uplink — SpaceX Starlink user terminals talking up to the satellites (the downlink shares 10.7–12.7 GHz).

name: Verizon 28 GHz
note: 28 GHz millimetre-wave (n261) — Verizon's high-capacity 5G Ultra Wideband in stadiums and dense downtowns.

name: 39 GHz mmWave
note: 39 GHz millimetre-wave (n260) — high-band 5G shared among T-Mobile, AT&T and Verizon.
